free_text: Cross sections of the production of mercury and radon
isotopes in complete fusion reactions with 36,40Ar
and 40,48Ca projectiles
Palacky University, Olomouc, Czech Republic
English translation
Russian original
The same data are given.
U-400 cyclotron
Beam intensities were no higher than 1.2x10**9
particles/s.
An upgraded catcher foil technique using a pile of
five thin aluminum foils 20 mm in diameter and 0.8
mu-m thick was used to make measurements. The pile of
foils was positioned at a distance of 18.2 mm behind
the target. Two additional aluminum foils 0.8 mu-m
thick were added immediately downstream of the target
in order to obtain the maximum yield of reaction
products in the middle of the pile.
Five silicon detectors with the energy resolution
from 70 to 100 keV (FWHM) were used.
Layers of 144Sm, 166Er, 148Sm, 142Nd, and 144Nd
280-330 mu-g/cm2 thick, deposited electrochemically
on titanium substrates 680 mu-g/cm2 thick, were used
as the targets.
Cross-section error included the statistical
error and the errors in determining the target's
thickness and the beam dose.
Error in determining the target's thickness
Error in determining the beam dose
*By compiler* The authors consider the data for 6n
channel in 144Sm+40Ar reaction to be preliminary.
Entry was approved by Dr. E.V.Chernysheva.
Reference code (Phys.Rev.C) was added.
